subtitle: All the news that's fit to Fingerprint

lead.headline: PubMatic Names MiQ Among First Partners In AI-In-The-Auction Layer
lead.dek: Decision Fabric lets approved buy-side models run as Docker containers inside AgenticOS bid requests, returning segment IDs in under ten milliseconds.
lead.body:
PubMatic on June 1 unveiled Decision Fabric, a containerised execution layer inside its AgenticOS auction infrastructure that lets curators, DSPs and algorithmic buyers deploy proprietary AI models as Docker containers receiving full bid requests in under 10 milliseconds. The first pilot cohort names MiQ alongside inPowered AI, Chalice AI and SWYM.AI, with a training set spanning more than 60 OpenRTB fields across web, mobile and CTV inventory in the US. PubMatic frames the build as the first programmable, ISO-27001-aligned environment for in-auction inference — partners' models live inside the SSP and act on the bid stream rather than receiving and re-bidding from outside.

For the independent programmatic stack, Decision Fabric is the week's most consequential partnership signal: it moves in-auction decisioning from a buy-side aspiration toward live SSP infrastructure, and it gives non-walled-garden demand a path to run proprietary models where the inventory lives. The open questions for buyers are which CTV supply paths Decision Fabric reaches first, how containerised models interact with PubMatic-curated deals, and whether the pattern offers a credible counter-narrative to The Trade Desk's OpenPath. The deeper test is portability — whether the same containerised approach translates to non-PubMatic SSPs as Magnite, FreeWheel and Index Exchange move toward agentic architectures.

stories.headline: Amazon Ships Dynamic TV Creative For Prime Video IVAs
stories.dek: Headlines, products and CTAs now vary at the impression on Prime Video Interactive Video Ads.
stories.body: Amazon Ads on June 1 launched Dynamic TV Creative, the first capability that automatically personalises Prime Video Interactive Video Ads using shopping, browsing and streaming signals against an AI-driven creative-optimisation layer. Headline, product detail, call-to-action and interactivity vary per impression; the tool ships first to US endemic advertisers and is slated to expand to live sports and Prime Video Channels in Q3. Amazon repeats its claim that its authenticated graph reaches roughly 90% of US households deterministically. By raising the bar on creative-level CTV personalisation, Amazon increases the pressure on the open programmatic stack: independent CTV demand now has to compete with the walled gardens on creative resolution as well as on inventory access, and the burden falls on the open market to show it can match impression-level creative variation.

stories.headline: Google Ads Switches CTV To Co-View Reach, Inflating YouTube TV Audiences
stories.dek: Seven Connected TV metrics now count every viewer on the couch as of June 2.
stories.body: Google Ads' API transitioned on June 2 to Total Co-view reach across seven Connected TV campaign measures, counting every viewer on a single screen rather than treating the household as one impression. YouTube CTV reach numbers are expected to climb materially as a result, with effective CPMs falling on paper even where dollar spend holds steady. The change lands as YouTube claims more than 200 million Americans now watch on connected TVs each month and CTV passed mobile as YouTube's largest US watch surface in Q4 2025. The moment buyers compare YouTube CTV against rival streamers, the math changes overnight — agencies and DSPs will need to normalise across co-view and household-level bases before the new numbers start showing up in plans, or risk apples-to-oranges comparisons across the open market.

stories.headline: OpenAP And Big Nine Publishers Stand Up Cross-Streamer Outcomes CAPI
stories.dek: A+E, AMC, Fox, Hallmark, NBCU, Paramount, Scripps, TelevisaUnivision and Warner Bros. Discovery line up on one conversion pipe.
stories.body: OpenAP and nine of the largest US TV companies — A+E Global Media, AMC, Fox, Hallmark Media, NBCUniversal, Paramount, Scripps Networks, TelevisaUnivision and Warner Bros. Discovery — partnered to introduce a standardised cross-publisher conversion API for TV advertising, plus shared workflows for unified audience buying across linear and streaming. First pilots with select advertisers, agencies and attribution providers are underway, with measurable results targeted over the next 12 months and an explicit agentic-workflow phase to follow. The publishers are openly framing the move as their answer to digital rivals' outcomes pitch. A single CAPI integration point across the Big Nine is the unlock buyers have been demanding — but it also raises the bar for every outcomes package in the market, because curators and DSPs now have to deliver against publisher-owned conversion infrastructure rather than around it.

stories.headline: Nexxen And V Wire Hisense Home Screen For World Cup Sponsor Inventory
stories.dek: OEM-direct native CTV placements across Hisense and Toshiba sets aimed at the global football audience.
stories.body: Nexxen and V — the OEM formerly known as VIDAA — said June 3 they will connect advertisers with global soccer fans during the FIFA World Cup 2026 via native Smart TV home-screen placements and exclusive activations on Hisense and Toshiba sets. Hisense is an official FIFA sponsor, giving Nexxen a privileged window into one of CTV's biggest tentpole audiences. The deal underlines the OEM-led inventory race ahead of World Cup spend. For buy-side strategists, the question before tournament-quarter briefs land is which DSPs and curators have native access to V/Hisense screens, and how OEM-direct home-screen inventory stacks up against the Nexxen pipe — OEM placements are increasingly a distinct line item that sits upstream of any streamer's ad pod.

stories.headline: FIFA+ Folds Into DAZN, Concentrating 8,500 Live Matches On One Streamer
stories.dek: Football streamer adds FIFA archive and originals weeks before World Cup kicks off.
stories.body: FIFA+ launched exclusively on DAZN on June 4, completing the integration first announced in June 2025 and marking what DAZN calls a major step toward becoming 'the global home of football.' The combined offer brings around 8,500 live matches a year from roughly 100 FIFA Member Associations, plus FIFA's archive and originals, onto DAZN. DAZN also holds 2026 World Cup rights in Italy, Japan and Spain. The move concentrates a vast volume of premium live football inventory inside one streaming front door just weeks before the tournament — reshaping the global Advanced TV plan for any brand chasing football audiences and routing more of that demand through DAZN's ad-supported tiers rather than across a fragmented rights map.

stories.headline: Dentsu Extends Magnite Pact Into Sweden For AMX Premium Video
stories.dek: Holding company hard-wires another EMEA market to an independent SSP.
stories.body: Magnite and dentsu announced on June 3 an extension of their existing EMEA partnership into Sweden, with dentsu using Magnite's programmatic infrastructure to power CTV buying for the agency's AMX Premium Video offering in the market. The extension builds on prior dentsu-Magnite work and on Magnite's recent SSP deals with Samsung Ads, Paramount Australia and Hearst. The pair frame the deal as enabling more intelligent, data-driven CTV activation. The deal is another holding company hard-wiring an independent SSP into its curated supply path — a useful reference point for how indie-SSP positioning plays across Nordic and DACH briefs, and a marker of how much premium-video buying in EMEA is consolidating onto a handful of independent supply paths.

stories.headline: The Trade Desk Ships Q2 Kokai With CTV Pause Ads And New AI Controls
stories.dek: Deal Desk upgrades and pause-format demand land as the streaming attention surface widens.
stories.body: The Trade Desk pushed its Q2 2026 Kokai release in early June with new AI optimisation controls, CTV pause-ad demand (ads delivered when viewers hit pause on streaming) and Deal Desk upgrades for buyers. The pause format opens a high-attention CTV moment that TTD's buyers can target via Kokai out of the box, the same week IAB Tech Lab is finalising standardised OpenRTB signals for Pause, Menu and Squeeze-Back formats. The open question for the rest of the market is which CTV publishers TTD has lined up for pause-ad supply, and whether curated deal IDs elsewhere in the ecosystem can match the offer — buy-side parity on emerging formats now depends on how fast independent supply paths align to the same standardised signals.

stories.headline: Trade Desk Wires Utiq Telco Identity Into EMEA Kokai Across Six Markets
stories.dek: Network-level signals from Deutsche Telekom, Orange, Telefonica and Vodafone join EUID and UID2.
stories.body: The Trade Desk and Utiq announced on June 1 an integration of Utiq's telco-powered, user-consented advertising identifiers — generated at the network layer by Deutsche Telekom, Orange, Telefonica and Vodafone — into TTD's EMEA identity portfolio, available via Kokai. Coverage spans Germany, Austria, France, Spain, the UK and Italy, including netID in Germany. TTD continues to broaden its non-cookie addressability story in Europe with telco signals layered on top of EUID and UID2. For independent demand in EMEA, telco-consent identity is a competitive flank to be reckoned with — particularly where Utiq's network-level consent posture lands cleaner than email-based alternatives, which sharpens the question of how the rest of the addressability stack augments or competes with operator-sourced signals.

stories.headline: New York Synthetic-Performer Disclosure Law Takes Effect June 9
stories.dek: First-in-nation rule forces AI-actor disclosure on ads served to NY consumers; $1,000 first-offence, $5,000 repeat.
stories.body: New York's S.8420-A/A.8887-B takes effect June 9, requiring advertisers with actual knowledge of AI-generated 'synthetic performers' in their creative to conspicuously disclose that use in ads reaching New York consumers, with $1,000 first-offence and $5,000 repeat penalties. Audio-only ads and pure language translation are exempt; the rule does not apply to publishers and platforms that distribute the ads. Legal commentary calls this the first state-level synthetic-performer disclosure regime in the country. New York is the largest US ad market and the first practical test of AI-creative disclosure plumbing through CTV inventory — agencies will look to their programmatic partners to flag and gate creative on intake, putting pressure on the whole supply chain to build disclosure controls into delivery.

stories.headline: CCPA Risk Assessments Now Required For Adtech And Sensitive Data
stories.dek: California's Audits Division gears up; Disney's $2.75M settlement set the tone.
stories.body: California's updated CCPA regulations effective January 1 require risk assessments before any 'significant risk' processing — including selling or sharing personal information for cross-context behavioural advertising — with first certifications due to the CPPA by April 2028. The CPPA's new Audits Division has telegraphed compliance audits through 2026, and the 2026 enforcement docket already includes a $2.75M Disney CCPA settlement plus actions against Ford and PlayOn Sports. Documented risk assessments are now table stakes for any addressable CTV activation touching California audiences — a compliance burden that buy-side strategists and their partners increasingly treat as a managed-service deliverable rather than a box-checking afterthought.

stories.headline: Kinetiq Pegs 2026 Political CTV At $2.7B As Streaming Crosses 25% Share
stories.dek: Cycle projection raised above $10B; CTV up 122% versus 2022, broadcast down 13%.
stories.body: Kinetiq Political Insights this week raised 2026 cycle projections above $10 billion in political media spend, with CTV pulling roughly $2.7 billion — a 122% jump over 2022 and the first cycle where streaming crosses 25% of total political ad spend. Broadcast and cable both decline versus 2024 in the model. California currently leads state-level spend at $315.8M, dominated by Tom Steyer's gubernatorial bid; AZ, GA, MI, NV, PA, NC and FL races round out the swing-state pipe. The streaming-share crossover is the line every CTV seller will quote at agencies through Q3 — and the buy side will want verified-supply and measurement answers to match before the new share figure becomes the default planning assumption for political dollars.

stories.headline: ICO Advises London To Limit PECR Exemption To Contextual Ads
stories.dek: Consent still required for cross-service tracking and behavioural profiling under regulator's proposal.
stories.body: The UK Information Commissioner's Office published advice in late May, picked up across early-June industry coverage, recommending that Regulation 6 of PECR be revised to distinguish low-risk ad practices from intrusive tracking, with a no-consent exemption limited to contextual advertising and similar models. Behavioural targeting and cross-service profiling would continue to require explicit consent under the ICO proposal; current PECR rules remain in force pending any legislative change. The regulator is publicly drawing a line between contextual and behavioural CTV models — strengthening the case for contextual and clean-room-based addressability in the British market and signalling where consent-free targeting can and cannot operate.

stories.headline: JioHotstar Deepens Magnite Pact Across 450M-MAU Sports And Entertainment Portfolio
stories.dek: SpringServe to mediate ads across live cricket, IPL and the JioStar streaming stack.
stories.body: JioHotstar said on June 4 it is expanding its partnership with Magnite, tapping SpringServe to drive advanced ad-server mediation across its 450M-MAU live sports and entertainment portfolio after Magnite already supported the platform through the ICC Men's T20 World Cup. The deepened deal gives the JioStar-owned service enhanced creative controls, brand-safety oversight and unified monetisation across CTV, mobile and web. The expansion lands the same week TAM Sports data showed CTV captured 39% of IPL 2026 digital ad share with JioHotstar alone accounting for 35% of top-advertiser volumes. The deal consolidates the largest non-US CTV inventory pool under an independent SSP that buyers can reach programmatically — a critical access point for any global IPL and ICC activation plan.

stories.headline: Globo Lines Up AI Ad-Decisioning For World Cup Globoplay Streams
stories.dek: Adtech director Beaklini sets out low-latency, hyper-personalised live insertion playbook.
stories.body: Globo's director of adtech Ana Beaklini detailed the broadcaster's World Cup 2026 live ad-insertion playbook in early June, leaning on low-latency delivery and AI-driven decisioning to hyper-personalise spots across Globoplay, TV Globo, SporTV and ge for the country's biggest single advertising event. The strategy combines TV 3.0 standards work with dynamic SSAI to address the historic latency gap on big-event live streams. Globo's AI ad-decisioning stack is the most advanced CTV signal coming out of Brazil ahead of the tournament and a likely template for TelevisaUnivision and other Latin American broadcasters — making programmatic access into Globoplay's SSAI rails a live question for any buyer planning around the region's biggest live event.

stories.headline: Ottawa Orders CRTC To Scrap 15% Streamer CanCon Levy, Promises $600M Backfill
stories.dek: Heritage cites consumer cost pressure and US trade friction in killing tripled foreign-streamer contribution.
stories.body: On June 3 the Canadian government directed the CRTC to review its decision that tripled foreign streamers' base contribution to Canadian-content funds from 5% to 15% of domestic revenue, promising a $600M taxpayer-funded backfill instead. Heritage cited consumer cost concerns and ongoing US trade pressure under the Online Streaming Act as it walked the levy back. The reversal protects Netflix, Disney+ and Amazon CTV ad-spend headroom heading into a heavy World Cup quarter while reshuffling the competitive balance against Bell, Rogers and Corus domestic AVOD — a structural shift the open market should price into 2026 H2 plans for the Canadian market.

stories.headline: China CTV Ad Spend Forecast To Reach $3.47B In 2026 Despite iQIYI Slip
stories.dek: Long-form streamers lean on AI tooling and OEM partnerships as Tencent and Mango eye AI-drama era.
stories.body: Industry forecasts peg China CTV ad spend at $3.47B in 2026, up 11.5% YoY, even as long-form leaders face turbulence — iQIYI's online ad revenue dropped 9% to RMB 5.19B and Mango TV's pre-announced net profit is set to fall 33-47% for 2025. Tencent Video and iQIYI executives now predict the first blockbuster AI-generated drama will arrive in H2 2026, opening a new content cost structure. For global strategy, the China CTV market remains gated for US-headquartered demand, but the AI-content shift and OEM-led FAST growth keep it on the watch list for any APAC plan where TCL, Hisense or Xiaomi inventory shows up — those OEM operating systems are where Chinese supply most often surfaces in Western buys.

stories.headline: The Auction Has Swallowed The Buyer
stories.dek: PubMatic's containers and Google's co-view are not products. They are the marketplace acquiring an interior life.
stories.body:
The figure this week is the agent. PubMatic ships a fabric that lets MiQ, inPowered, Chalice and SWYM run their models as Docker containers inside the bid request — under ten milliseconds, sixty fields, a quiet little soul nested in the auction. The Trade Desk pushes pause-ad controls into Kokai. Google Ads quietly redefines a reach metric so that every co-viewer on a couch is counted. Olyzon, freshly funded by Sir Martin, ships five named agents — Map, Score, Adapt, Activate, Prove — to orchestrate the planners. That is the figure. The figure is not the story. The ground is the auction itself, which has grown an interior, and the buyer who used to stand outside it bidding has been quietly moved inside.

*Enhance:* speed and reach — the model executes where the inventory lives. *Obsolesce:* the trader as a person sending bids from a chair; the open marketplace where buyer and seller faced one another across an exchange floor. *Retrieve:* the medieval bourse, where the buyer, the goods and the broker were all in one stone room and the negotiation was conducted in glances. *Reverse:* pushed far enough, the auction has nothing to deliver to, because the buyer is no longer in the room — he is the room.

We march backwards into the future. The marketer who once selected an audience now writes a small program and ships it through a port. The agent is warm in a strange new way. Google's co-view metric is the same gesture in lower resolution: the medium constructs the audience it claims to measure. Joyce of course knew this in 1939. The agent is the content.

*— Marshall McLuhan*

stories.headline: No Agent Can Save A Bad Spot
stories.dek: The auction has acquired a brain. The creative has a brief from 1985.
stories.body:
I have read this week of Decision Fabric, of Kokai's new AI knobs, of Dynamic TV Creative on Prime Video, of five named agents from Paris orchestrating the DSPs. The trade has not been this excited since it discovered the word 'incrementality'. Then on Wednesday TripleLift, which I had to ask my secretary to spell, published a study. Forty-nine percent of connected-television spots are still the fifteen- and thirty-second cuts our trade was making for the antenna in 1976. The streamers reach seventy-four percent of American television viewing. Forty-nine percent of the creative running through this glittering new pipework was built for a medium none of these viewers are watching. I call it a discipline problem.

In 1951 Ellerton Jette of the Hathaway shirt company paid me thirty thousand dollars and gave me a free hand. The company had not advertised in 116 years. On the way to the photograph I stopped at a chemist on Lexington Avenue and bought an eyepatch for a dollar fifty. The campaign ran for twenty-one years. Hathaway sold out in a week and posted thirty-six percent more shirts in the first quarter. The eyepatch was an idea. No fabric of containers, no auction, no Docker, no five-agent decisioning rig will generate it for you. Ideas come from the discipline of looking at the product and the consumer until something true appears.

If half of the spots running through these magnificent new rails are linear in disguise, then the cleverness of the bidding tower is academic. Sell the product. Build the brand. Test the work. Buy the receipt. And for God's sake, before you ask an agent to optimise a spot, ask whether the spot is worth showing. We sell, or else.

*— David Ogilvy*

what_to_watch: Whether OpenAP's cross-publisher CAPI begins surfacing first-pilot results before Q3 upfronts close — the moment the Big Nine's outcomes story moves from press release to delivered measurement is the moment the independent-measurement market reprices.
